data.title: Vertex upgraded to buy by Jefferies on fundamentals, pipeline
data.description:
• Jefferies has upgraded Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) to buy from hold, citing improving fundamentals and the company’s late-stage pipeline drugs for pain and APOL1-mediated kidney disease.
  • The investment bank said it likes the recent pullback in the stock and sees “$5-7B+ of pipeline coming in the next leg of growth in 2026-2030.”
  • “VRTX (VRTX) is a clean growth name and should continue to be owned and favored by generalists and long onlies based on solid fundamentals and a growing pipeline, and as part of a scarce growth asset within large biopharma with no major data risk in 2025, no obesity volatility or exposure, etc.,” said Jefferies in a note.
  • Jefferies raised its price target for the stock to $550 from $500.

data.title: HIG Europe submits offer for Kantar Media -Sky News
data.description:
• Private equity firm HIG Europe submitted a formal bid for Kantar Media, which is reportedly valued at about £1b.
  • There's no certainty that a deal will happen, according to a Sky News item on Monday.
  • Kantar Media measures ratings data in many countries around the world. Kantar Media is a unit of Kantar Group, which is majority-owned by PE firm Bain Capital. WPP Plc (NYSE:WPP) has a minority stake in Kantar Media.
  • Last week, France's Ipsos (OTCPK:IPSOF) was considering a bid for Kantar Media, thought it dropped out of the process, according to the report.

data.title: Newell Brands' turnaround went unnoticed by investors - analyst
data.description:
The “heavy lifting” of the company’s turnaround is complete, but no one was watching, says Truist analyst Bill Chappell, who upgraded Newell Brands (NASDAQ:NWL) to Buy from Hold and launched the shares to a 17-month-high.

“Investors have largely overlooked the turnaround efforts over the past four years due to the volatility created by the pandemic and post-pandemic behavior,” Chappell says, adding that investors should take notice of the company’s potential with organic top-line growth now on the horizon, a key metric for consumer product stocks.

Since Chris Peterson took the helm of the parent company of Graco, Sharpie, Rubbermaid, and Yankee Candle, there have been two restructuring programs, new business division heads for 75% of the businesses, an 80% reduction in the SKU count, 350 basis point gross margin improvement, and $416M reduction in total debt. Given these turnaround efforts occurred during volatile swings in its core categories, investors were not paying close attention to these impressive accomplishments.

“In our opinion, 2025 will be the first ‘normal’ operating environment for the company since 2019 enabling the turnaround efforts to be more apparent to investors,” said Chappell.

Initially, Chappell did not view the outlook for increased tariffs from China as a positive for Newell Brands (NWL) until the company highlighted the issue at an investor's conference last week. But restrictions on Chinese imports might actually benefit Newell (NWL) as roughly half of its products are manufactured in the U.S. and compete against Chinese-made brands, while only 15% of its current products are sourced from China, expected to drop to just 10% by the end of next year. Additionally, the majority of those products still sourced in China are baby related, exempt from the previous round of tariffs.

“Admittedly, we are uncertain how tariffs will play out over the coming months, but we do not believe the stock reflects the company being a potential BENEFICIARY of increased tariffs,” Chappell says.

Besides an upgrade, Chappell hikes his target price for Newell Brands (NWL) by 70% to $17, a 65% upside to Friday’s close.

Shares were up nearly 12% into Monday’s close, it’s largest one-day gain since July.

data.title: Expand Energy raised to Buy at UBS, citing right leverage to rising natgas prices
data.description:
Expand Energy (NASDAQ:EXE) +1.1% in Monday's trading as UBS upgrades shares to Buy from Neutral with a $123 price target, raised from $94, saying the company's ability to ramp volumes into a rising natural gas price environment is a strategic advantage vs. peers, with at least 1.5B cfe/day of built-up productive capacity.

UBS analyst Josh Silverstein also sees Expand (EXE) ramping shareholder returns by mid-2025 after retiring $389M of debt in the upcoming Q1.

Silverstein also expects natural gas E&P valuations will re-rate to reflect the improving long-term domestic demand outlook from liquefied natural gas exports; while some of this already has occurred, the analyst views Expand (EXE) as having more upside relative to peers.

Downside risks include widening differentials, further delays in the LNG export facility buildout, and the integration of Southwestern assets, but Expand (EXE) already has increased its synergy target to $500M from $400M, with $225M to be captured in 2025, reducing the risk.

data.title: Incyte reports positive Phase 3 data for Zynyz for lung cancer
data.description:
Incyte (NASDAQ:INCY) reported positive results from a Phase 3 study of its drug Zynyz in the treatment of non-small cell lung cancer, or NSCLC.

The study tested Zynyz, also known as retifanlimab, in combination with platinum-based chemotherapy for the treatment of previously untreated non-squamous and squamous metastatic NSCLC not harboring a driver mutation.

Incyte (INCY) said the study met its primary endpoint of overall survival and all secondary endpoints, according to a statement.

Incyte (INCY) presented the data on Saturday at the European Society for Medical Oncology Asia Congress 2024 in Singapore. The company plans to use the data to support a supplemental Biologics License Application for retifanlimab in the treatment of NSCLC.

The drug is already approved for the treatment of Merkel cell carcinoma.

data.title: Restaurant retreat: CAVA Group and Sweetgreen fall sharply after monster 2024 rallies
data.description:
Two of the top-performing restaurant stocks of 2024 fell sharply on Monday as investors pulled back on CAVA Group (NYSE:CAVA) and Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG).

Some traders are pointing to an SEC filing late on Friday from CAVA (CAVA) co-founder Theodoros Xenohristos as sparking the sell-off. Xenohristos disclosed that he recently sold $9.48 million worth of shares in a series of transactions.

Shares of CAVA Group (CAVA) fell 10.9% in late afternoon trading to cut into the +200% year-to-date rally. Sweetgreen (SG) was down 10.5% after more than doubling this year. Volume on both restaurant stocks was above normal activity.

Short interest in Sweetgreen (SG) is at 13.2% of the total float, while short interest in CAVA Group (CAVA) is at 9.5%. Sweetgreen (SG) still has a Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Buy, while the quantitative analysis on CAVA Group (CAVA) has the stock slotted at Hold.

data.title: The case for Grid Electrification for 2025 – Barclays
data.description:
One of Barclays’s “wish lists” for 2025 is for policies that expand and modernize electric grid infrastructure for the growing electricity demand.

With only 55 miles of new high-voltage transmission capacity build in the U.S. last year, there needs to be a two-time or three-time increase in U.S. transmission capacity by 2050 to meet demand growth and reliability needs, according to a U.S. Department of Energy's National Transmission Planning study.

The study said that this expansion could save about $270-490B in energy costs through 2050.

In addition, the International Energy Agency has forecasted that grid spending needs to reach over $600B per year by 2030 for countries to implement their national targets.

“Increasing electricity demand driven by AI/data centers, reshoring of manufacturing, broad electrification and cryptocurrencies is precipitating investment in grid infrastructure and has fueled growing political momentum for infrastructure permit reform,” wrote Hiral Patel, head of Sustainable & Thematic Investing at Barclays, in a note.

“However, the fate of FERC Order 1920, which seeks to expedite transmission development and ensure grid reliability in the U.S., may also be at risk under the Trump administration.”

Shortages of critical grid equipment, including power transformers and switchgear, could also delay grid expansion, Patel added.

Barclays analysts highlighted 17 major “pick and shovel” global electrification companies that have high exposure to this theme.

Here they are with their Seeking Alpha quant ratings:

  • ABB Ltd. (OTCPK:ABBNY) – Quant rating: 3.68
  • Alfen N.V. (OTCPK:ALFNF) – Not rated.
  • Eaton Corp. (ETN) – Quant rating: 3.37
  • Fluence Energy Inc. (FLNC) – Quant rating: 2.75
  • GE Vernova Inc. (GEV) – Not rated.
  • Hitachi Ltd. (OTCPK:HTHIY) – Quant rating: 3.09
  • Hubbell Inc. (HUBB) – Quant rating: 3.24
  • MasTec Inc. (MTZ) – Quant rating: 4.79
  • Nexans S.A. (OTCPK:NXPRF) – Not rated.
  • NKT A/S (OTCPK:NRKBF) - Not rated.

data.title: HF Sinclair, PBF Energy downgraded at Wells Fargo on 2025 outlook
data.description:
Mid-cap refiners HF Sinclair (NYSE:DINO) and PBF Energy (NYSE:PBF) are downgraded Monday at Equal Weight from Overweight with respective $45 and $34 price targets at Wells Fargo, even as the bank remains positive overall on U.S. refining (CRAK) while anticipating "a year of two halves" for the industry - weaker up front, stronger in the back.

Wells Fargo analyst Roger Read says HF Sinclair (DINO) has done a solid job transforming to a more operational and geographically diverse company, but the continued weak performance of its RD operations is a bit of a headwind for improved earnings and returns.

PBF Energy (PBF) looks inexpensive but Read sees minimal prospects for wider crude differentials through mid-2025 as OPEC is likely to restrain production through mid-year; the analyst continues to view PBF's improved balance sheet and key position on the West Coast as advantaged and direct exposure to the East Coast remains a positive given below five-year average distillate inventory levels.

The Wells Fargo team reiterates Outperform ratings for large-cap refiners Marathon Petroleum (MPC), Phillips 66 (PSX) and Valero Energy (VLO).

data.title: Zeta Global tumbles on Interpublic-Omnicom merger; CEO issues statement
data.description:
• Zeta Global (NYSE:ZETA) shares tumbled more than 10% on Monday after Monday's mega-merger in the advertising sector between Interpublic (NYSE:IPG) and Omnicom (NYSE:OMC), prompting Zeta chief David Steinberg to issue a statement.
  • “Expanding on my earlier comments, we are proud of our extensive relationships with the top Holdcos, including both Omnicom and IPG, and believe that today’s announcement is a positive one for the industry and Zeta," Steinberg said. "Like everyone else, we will be watching closely as this progresses and offer support as needed.”

data.title: OpenAI launches Sora, AI-powered video creator
data.description:
Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI launched Sora, an artificial intelligence-powered video generator that can create videos with as little as a few words of text.

"Video is important to OpenAi for a lot of reasons," said OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. "This is a new co-creative dynamic for our creators. We want our AI to understand video creations, and it will get better and better as it learns."

"This is going live today in most of the world, except for Europe and the U.K.," Altman added. "It might be a while before we can operate it there."

Users start on the Explore page, which is a feed of videos shared by the community. A box at the bottom of each video explains how it was created. Videos can be created via a line of text or using an image to kickstart the project.

There is also a more advanced tool called Storyboard. The creator can arrange a series of cards, with each card describing a specific scene. Storyboard then melds these instructions together into a video.

Sora was launched on Day 3 of "12 days of OpenAI."

On Day 1, OpenAI announced ChatGPT Pro while Day 2 highlighted its Reinforcement Fine-Tuning Research Program.
